concept: PFAS-free pumped two-phase direct-to-chip cooling loop: hermetic, fluid-agnostic evaporator/condenser subsystem co-qualified with low-GWP non-PFAS working fluids
named_buyer_examples: Chinese liquid-cooling system vendors qualifying to T/CIEP 0263-2025 (CN)
Chinese AI-datacenter operators under GB 40879 PUE limits (CN)
painful_job: US: two-phase cooling is the path past single-phase cold plates for 3-5kW chips (L14-010, L14-001/002/003), but 3M's PFAS exit removed the qualified fluid family while the AIM Act tightens HFCs 2029-2033 (L14-033) - vendors have no fluid-plus-loop combination they can bank. CN: GB 40879 pushes PUE to <=1.25/1.2 (L14-035), T/CIEP 0263-2025 codifies liquid cooling (L14-039), and Chinese reviews call phase-change cooling the trajectory (L14-029), with the same global fluid vacuum.
product: Primary variant (merged from P3R2-B-01): a sealed negative-pressure (subatmospheric) two-phase loop - saturation temperature set by vacuum setpoint; seal failures leak air inward (vacuum-decay detected) rather than coolant onto boards; PFAS-free by design on water or low-GWP fluids (ITRI-class low-pressure results, L14-053). Second moat: the fluid-menu qualification dataset - cold plate + micro-pump + condenser + charge management co-qualified across a menu of non-PFAS, low-GWP fluids. Sold to ODM/CDU vendors as a drop-in >2kW/chip module with fluid-lifetime warranty data.
frontier_vision: Own the two-phase thermal layer for the 5kW-chip era across datacenters, power electronics, EV fast-charging, and radar - fluid-chemistry-independent, so every future regulatory shock becomes our upgrade cycle.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: fluid screening matrix with a chemical partner (Chemours/Honeywell-class), building the post-Novec qualification dataset the literature lacks (L14-022); patents on charge management and fluid-agnostic evaporator design. 2027: decisive 2kW demo (below). 2028: 6-month reliability run + hyperscaler lab evaluation. 2029: ODM design-in LOI; refresh fluid regulatory status (AIM Act step, EU F-gas) before launch.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Regulatory clocks (AIM Act 30% step 2029, L14-033; EU F-gas, L14-034) collide with 3-5kW chips right at 2030; incumbents bet on single-phase water or on discontinued PFAS fluids, leaving a certified PFAS-free two-phase loop unowned at launch.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: EPA AIM Act HFC allowance step-down to 30% of baseline for 2029-2033 (L14-033) plus next-gen accelerator TDPs >4kW (L14-046 context, L14-010). CN: GB 40879 revision enforcement (PUE <=1.25/1.2, L14-035) and T/CIEP 0263-2025 adoption cycle (L14-039) as new-campus acceptance criteria.
competition_outlook_2030: US: ZutaCore (waterless two-phase, $100M+ C, L14-046), Accelsius, LiquidStack/Trane (L14-051), JetCool/Flex microconvective (L14-045), Motivair/Schneider CDUs. CN: Envicool (Deschutes CDU vendor list, L14-043), Huawei in-house. Differentiator: fluid-agnostic qualification data, not another proprietary fluid bet.
timing_window_risk: Fluid availability is the binding risk - if no non-PFAS two-phase fluid reaches datacenter-scale qualification by 2031, single-phase water wins another cycle (kill trigger). Export separability good: thermal hardware is uncontrolled; CN entity can localize manufacturing. Do not rely on the unconfirmed Chinese '100% liquid-cooling mandate' (contradicted by primary text, L14-036). FIX: the 2031 fluid-availability kill trigger is retained and extended to cover the subatmospheric-water variant.
extreme_edge: Stable flow-boiling heat removal above 500 W/cm2 hotspot flux in a sealed, orientation-tolerant loop with non-engineered-fluid chemistries.
first_experiment: 2027: 2kW cold-plate pumped two-phase loop running two candidate non-PFAS fluids; map heat-flux/stability envelope vs Novec-era published baselines; 1,000h sealed-loop degradation assay.
founder_fit_note: Fluid-property auto-identification, vacuum-decay detection, and loop control electronics fit founder sensing/controls skills, while the core two-phase cold-plate, micro-pump, and fluid-qualification work is thermal-fluids and chemistry outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: adopted B-01's negative-pressure architecture as the primary loop mechanism (defensible-by-physics wedge) with the fluid-menu qualification dataset as second moat; 2031 fluid-availability kill trigger retained; confidence high->medium pending the mechanism demo. B-01 remains the CN-primary canonical; C-04 carries the US+CN dual variant.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): As written a positioning play vs funded two-phase startups; regulatory triggers genuinely independent per country.

concept: Standards-grade thermal emulation and conformance metrology: programmable multi-kW chip-scale thermal test vehicles and rack-scale load emulators for liquid-cooling qualification
primary_customer_archetype: CDU/cold-plate vendors, ODMs, datacenter commissioning agents
named_buyer_examples: The 7+ vendors building CDUs to Google's Deschutes spec (Boyd, CoolerMaster, Delta, Envicool, Nidec, nVent, Vertiv) (US spec ecosystem)
Chinese liquid-cooling vendors certifying to T/CIEP 0263-2025 (CN)
Chinese datacenter operators proving GB 40879 PUE compliance (CN)
fluid makers (Chemours/Honeywell archetype) qualifying post-PFAS fluids (from A-15)
Chinese certification/inspection labs adopting T/CIEP 0263-2025 (from B-02)
painful_job: US: cooling vendors design against inconsistent secondary-source thermal specs - even GB200-class TDP/flow figures conflict across sources and NVIDIA's public page does not resolve them (L14-044) - so over/under-engineering risk lands on CDU vendors building to open specs (L14-043). CN: T/CIEP 0263-2025 and tightening PUE limits create conformance/acceptance-test demand with no standard test hardware (L14-039, L14-035).
product: Calibrated silicon/vapor-chamber thermal test vehicles (0.2-5 kW, programmable hotspot maps), manifold/rack emulators to 2 MW class, and traceable test protocols for liquid/two-phase cooling acceptance - the 'thermal dynamometer' for the industry. Merged extensions: precision flow/enthalpy calorimetry benches specified to <=1% energy-balance closure and accelerated post-PFAS fluid-qualification rigs (materials compatibility, dielectric aging) (from A-15); automated clause-by-clause conformance reporting against T/CIEP 0263-2025 plus a sell-to-certification-labs channel (from B-02).
frontier_vision: Become the JEDEC-era-defining metrology vendor for liquid-cooled computing: every cold plate, CDU, fluid, and TIM qualified on our vehicles, with the reference dataset feeding standards bodies in US, CN, and OCP.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: build first 1.2kW programmable test vehicle in a university lab; publish an open cross-vendor cold-plate comparison to establish methodology authority; engage OCP cooling workstream and Chinese standards ecosystem via an academic partner. 2028: rack-emulator pilot with one CDU vendor; protocol whitepaper. 2029: pre-orders/LOIs from 3 vendors; refresh chip-power roadmaps (2026 TDP figures will be stale).
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Cooling hardware revenue is exploding (Vertiv +50% liquid-cooling orders, $15B backlog, L14-048) while test methodology lags chips by two generations; by 2030, warranty and PUE-compliance stakes force third-party-grade qualification - a picks-and-shovels position with tiny capex.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: OCP-driven multi-vendor CDU/cold-plate proliferation (L14-043) plus liquid-cooling M&A integration waves (L14-045, L14-051) creating qualification bottlenecks 2030-2032. CN: T/CIEP 0263-2025 conformance adoption and GB 40879 PUE acceptance testing for national-hub datacenters (L14-039, L14-035).
competition_outlook_2030: US: in-house dummy-load rigs, thermal test houses; no dominant merchant vendor today. CN: institute labs; ITRI in Taiwan builds capability (L14-053) and NTT runs a verification hub in Japan (L14-050) - institutional analogs, not product companies. First credible product vendor can set the method.
timing_window_risk: Modest market-size ceiling is the real risk (instrumentation TAM), mitigated by consumables/protocol licensing; export separability excellent (test equipment, dual-entity trivial). If OCP or JEDEC ships a full method + reference hardware by 2030 without us, differentiation collapses - monitor via standards participation.
extreme_edge: Emulating 1,000 W/cm2-class transient hotspot maps with traceable calorimetry at rack scale - metrology fidelity nobody currently sells.
first_experiment: Q4 2026-2027: 1.2kW programmable thermal test vehicle with 16-zone hotspot control; blind round-robin of 3 commercial cold plates; publish method + variance data.
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: programmable thermal test vehicles, calorimetry benches with tight energy-balance closure, and traceable acceptance protocols are instrumentation, data-acquisition, controls, and numerical-analysis products in the founder's core skill set.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-15 (post-PFAS fluid-qualification rigs; <=1%-closure calorimetry spec; AIM Act/EU F-gas demand ids L14-033/034) and B-02 (T/CIEP 0263-2025 clause-scripted reporting; certification-lab channel; corrosion/biofouling protocol source L14-028). CN leg unchanged - already US+CN with T/CIEP-0263/GB-40879 evidence.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical thermal metrology. Documented spec chaos as demand driver; independent US/CN legs; tiny capex; TAM ceiling honestly flagged. Absorb A-15 and B-02.

concept: Low-ripple active-front-end rectification retrofits for electrochemical plants: IGBT/MMC power-quality upgrades that recover the documented 5-14% specific-energy penalty of thyristor supplies
primary_customer_archetype: electrolyzer plant operators, electrowinning tank houses
named_buyer_examples: DOE H2Hubs projects (HyVelocity Gulf Coast, MachH2) (US)
US copper electrowinning operators (rectifier-quality lever per vendor evidence)
painful_job: US: hydrogen projects financed under H2Hubs carry 15-20-year opex sensitivity to rectifier ripple - the literature documents up to ~14% specific-energy and ~13% loss penalties for six-pulse thyristor supplies vs transistor-based conversion (L11-016/017/019/020/021), and the same lever exists in electrowinning (L11-045, L11-026/027). CN: flagship plants underperform - Kuqa reportedly ran at a fraction of nameplate (L11-049) - while alkaline mega-tenders (L11-050/051) install cost-optimized thyristor rectification at GW scale.
product: Containerized active-front-end/MMC rectifier retrofit skids (5-50 MW class) with ripple/harmonic control tuned to stack electrochemistry, plus measurement-based performance contracts; stack-vendor-neutral. Merged extensions: per-cell/per-stack telemetry with a financier-grade measurement-and-verification (M&V) protocol backing the performance contracts (from A-19); electrochemistry-aware, degradation-weighted current scheduling that maps renewable variability into stack-safe trajectories (from B-13).
frontier_vision: Precision DC as a product: the power-quality layer for all electrochemistry - hydrogen, electrowinning, chlor-alkali, battery formation - capturing the efficiency margin chemistry vendors leave on the table.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: instrumented ripple-vs-degradation study on a pilot electrolyzer with a university/RTO partner (extends L11-001/003/004/021/031); publish the retrofit-economics model. 2027: decisive side-by-side experiment (below). 2028: MW-scale retrofit pilot MOU with one US project developer and one CN EPC via academic consortium. 2029: refresh policy (45V status), tender prices, and Chinese rectifier-vendor landscape before incorporation. Beachhead sequencing (from A-19): US copper-electrowinning tankhouses first (policy-independent energy lever), H2Hubs second.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Stack overcapacity (China ~2x demand, L11-035/051) makes stacks a commodity while electricity is the cost driver - exactly the environment where a 5-14% energy lever sells; by 2030 both markets have enough installed thyristor base to retrofit and enough new-build to spec-in.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: H2Hubs Phase-2 construction disbursements and commissioning 2027-2032 (L11-032) plus electrowinning energy-cost programs. CN: post-2030 second-wave green-H2 base buildouts and retrofit of underperforming first-wave plants (L11-049), with tender cadence evidenced by L11-050/051.
competition_outlook_2030: US 2030: ABB/Siemens/Ingeteam rectifier lines (industry incumbents - names not in atlas, flagged for P4), OEM-bundled power (Siemens Energy L11-040, Accelera L11-041). CN 2030: Sungrow - simultaneously buyer and formidable power-electronics competitor (L11-051) - plus domestic rectifier makers. Wedge: stack-neutral performance contracts and electrochemistry-specific ripple control.
timing_window_risk: US hydrogen demand is policy-fragile (order books already shrinking at Western OEMs, L11-047/048) - electrowinning beachhead de-risks this; China side risks Sungrow verticalization. Export separability good (industrial rectifiers uncontrolled; CN entity localizes). Kill if neither market yields a paid MW-scale retrofit by end-2033.
extreme_edge: Sub-1% current ripple at 100 kA-class DC delivery with per-cell-string modulation matched to catalyst degradation physics.
first_experiment: 2027: side-by-side SEC measurement on a pilot alkaline/PEM stack under six-pulse thyristor vs AFE supply across load profiles; publish verified %-savings band vs the L11-016/021 literature claims.
founder_fit_note: Ripple/harmonic control design and measurement-and-verification telemetry draw on founder controls, instrumentation, and data-acquisition skills, but 5-50 MW rectifier hardware, plant integration, and electrochemical process knowledge are outside the profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-19 (US tankhouse-first/H2Hub-second beachhead sequencing; financier M&V protocol; per-cell telemetry) and B-13 (Kuqa retrofit evidence already cited at L11-049; degradation-weighted current-scheduling mechanism with sources L11-004/012/013/031). Flags unchanged (already US+CN); Sungrow verticalization remains the CN kill condition; incumbent rectifier names still to be sourced in P4.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical rectification retrofit; the one L11 play the atlas licenses. Electrowinning beachhead hedges hydrogen policy fragility; Sungrow verticalization is CN kill condition; incumbent rectifier names to source in P4.

concept: Thermal-shock-tolerant printed-circuit heat exchangers (PCHE): merchant diffusion-bonded recuperators for sCO2 power, heat pumps, and hydrogen precooling
primary_customer_archetype: power-cycle and heat-pump OEMs, EPC integrators
named_buyer_examples: CNNC/CAS-IET sCO2 projects and steel-sintering retrofit fleet (CN, via licensed entity)
TC Energy-Hanwha pipeline waste-heat lineage (US/KR, L04-047, from E-08)
painful_job: US: PCHE recuperators are the most-cited sCO2 bottleneck; pilot integrators build bespoke units in-house (L04-039, L04-029, L04-004/005/006), and advanced-reactor BOP components come from a tiny qualified-vendor pool (L04-045). CN: China commercialized waste-heat sCO2 first (Chaotan One 30 MW, L04-048/051) with a state-media-estimated ~300-unit steel-sintering retrofit fleet (single-source figure, flagged) and CAS-IET actively researching thermal-cycling-tolerant PCHEs (L04-029) - i.e., no merchant supplier meets the rapid-cycling spec in either market.
product: Diffusion-bonded microchannel recuperators engineered for >20 C/min thermal transients (the L04-029 target), with creep/fatigue qualification per ASTM E139 (L04-112) and Sandia-documented materials selections (L04-113); sold as certified cores to cycle OEMs. Merged extensions: sold as catalog frames with published delivery times, not EPC subprojects (from A-12); graded-channel cores with compliant headers distributing transient thermal strain away from bond planes (from B-07); the acceptance-test protocol positioned to substitute for the missing sCO2 performance-test standard (from E-08).
frontier_vision: The Intel of high-temperature compact heat exchange: standard cores across sCO2 power, industrial heat pumps, thermal storage, hydrogen liquefaction precooling, and fusion-blanket-adjacent heat trains.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: materials/bonding research via university + national-lab user facility; subscale core fabrication; publish cycling-durability data addressing the L04-029 gap. 2028: decisive cycling experiment (below); begin ASME code-case conversations. 2029: supply LOIs with one US cycle developer and one CN licensee for the retrofit market; refresh China retrofit-fleet economics from eligible sources (current figure is single-source state media). Added task (from E-08): pre-commitment triangulation of the CN retrofit TAM from eligible sources - the RMB100bn-class figure stays single-source-flagged until then. Structural pairing (adjudication): develop jointly with P3R2-C-19 as one high-temperature-components company.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: US pilots (STEP scale-up, NET-Power lineage, advanced reactors post-NRC Part 53) and China's first-set-designated sCO2 rollouts both hit equipment-ordering phases 2030-2034; the recuperator is the choke-point component with documented unresolved specs, ideal for a focused merchant entrant.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: advanced-reactor BOP procurement following NRC Part 53 (effective 2026-04, L04-031) and post-STEP commercial sCO2 units (L04-039); DOE LPO-backed long-duration storage builds (L04-114). CN: CNNC Xinjiang molten-salt+sCO2 completion (~2028, L04-051) triggering 'first-set' follow-on procurement and steel-sintering retrofit wave.
competition_outlook_2030: Heatric/VPE-class incumbent PCHE makers (industry names, not in atlas - flag for P4), OEM in-house shops (L04-039), Chinese institute-affiliated fabricators (L04-029). Differentiation: qualified rapid-transient tolerance + code documentation, not capacity.
timing_window_risk: Export separability: heat exchangers for CN steel/waste-heat retrofits are low-sensitivity; nuclear-qualified US product line kept in US entity (no CN transfer). Risks: sCO2 commercialization slips (kill if no >=10MW-class merchant order by end-2033); Chinese institutes verticalize; manufacturing capex heavier than electronics seeds. All 2026 project schedules must be re-verified 2029.
extreme_edge: Surviving 500-700 C, 20+ MPa, and >20 C/min transients for 20-year life in a bonded microchannel core - simultaneous creep, fatigue, and corrosion frontier (L04-113).
first_experiment: 2028: subscale diffusion-bonded core; 1,000-cycle rapid-transient rig at >=20 C/min with 550 C peaks; post-test sectioning + creep data vs ASTM E139; publish.
secondary_markets: KR: KAERI/KAIST/Jinsol sCO2 consortium and Hanwha Power Systems as first-adopter partners and channel (L04-028, L04-047) [from E-08]
JP: MHI-owned Turboden ORC channel as adjacent heat-to-power distribution relationship (L04-044) [from E-08]
founder_fit_note: Low direct fit: diffusion-bonded heat-exchanger cores are a materials and thermo-mechanical product, with founder skills applying only peripherally to test-loop instrumentation and creep/fatigue data acquisition.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-12 (catalog-frames/lead-time framing, STEP/TerraPower buyer detail, creep-test source L04-101), B-07 (graded-channel/compliant-header mechanism, CNNC first-set logic; RMB100bn TAM stays single-source-flagged), E-08 (KR KAERI/Jinsol/Hanwha consortium channel, MHI-Turboden adjacency, CN-TAM triangulation task). Flags unchanged (already US+CN).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical PCHE. Independent demand legs (STEP/Part 53 vs Chaotan One/Xinjiang); cycling qualification + code documentation wedge; CN TAM single-source-flagged pending P4; absorb A-12, B-07, E-08.

concept: Standardized solid-state pulsed-power platform: stackable Marx/modulator modules with digital droop control for industrial e-beam, X-ray, and accelerator RF drive
named_buyer_examples: DOE ARDAP ecosystem (29 companies funded FY2023) (US)
SLAC GREEN-RF commercialization lineage / DOE facility RF upgrades (US, from C-20)
KR channel: Vitzro Nextec / Pohang Accelerator Laboratory klystron-localization ecosystem as manufacturing partner and validation site (L05-037, from E-05)
painful_job: US: cargo-screening linac demand is growing (+25% YoY segment, >$55M new orders, L05-033) and EtO-replacement e-beam capacity is duopoly-constrained with backlogs (L05-028), while every project re-engineers bespoke, non-interoperable modulators (five incompatible designs documented, L05-003/005/006/007/008; no dedicated acceptance standard beyond IEC 60060, L05-043). CN: CGN Dasheng signed RMB340M in H1-2025 accelerator orders (+21%, L05-035) and national isotope-center cyclotron tenders are live (L05-036) - the same fragmented drive-chain problem at larger unit volume.
product: A qualified family of stackable solid-state pulse modules (10-100 kV, kA-class, us-ms pulses) with digital droop correction and standard mechanical/control interfaces, sold as the drive chain to beam-system OEMs - 'the Vicor of pulsed power'. Merged product-family extensions: a published open module/interconnect interface spec offered as a de facto standard with N+2 hot-swap redundancy - the 'OCP of pulsed power' move (from D-05); hot-swap serviceability spec of module swap <30 min (from E-05); one qualified 3 kV brick, series-stacked with per-stage droop correction and fiber-synchronized gate timing (from B-09); and a high-efficiency GaN SSPA RF-drive line (20-100kW envelope-tracking blocks, ~70%+ chain-efficiency target) for accelerator and industrial cavity drive (from C-20).
frontier_vision: Make pulsed power a catalog engineering discipline: e-beam sterilization, cargo scanning, medical linacs, food processing, fusion-adjacent test stands, and eventually klystron-replacement drive for compact accelerators, all on one module family.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: reference-design work + published interoperability spec proposal (leveraging the L05-009 standardization thread); patents on module droop-sharing control. 2027: decisive module demo (below). 2028: OEM evaluation units via lab partnership; acceptance-test protocol aligned to IEC 60060-1:2025 (L05-043). 2029: two OEM design-in LOIs (one US NDT/sterilization, one CN industrial e-beam); refresh backlog/tender data.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: EtO regulatory pressure and customs screening keep beam demand compounding in both countries while the supply chain remains artisanal; a standardized module vendor entering 2030 rides OEM capacity expansion instead of competing with it.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: EtO-replacement sterilization capacity buildout (duopoly backlog through 2026-27 today, L05-028 - re-verify 2029) and customs/ports scanning recapitalization (L05-033). CN: CGN Dasheng multi-site expansion cadence (L05-035) and isotope-center accelerator procurement (L05-036). Acceptance-protocol emphasis (from A-07): the published acceptance-test protocol aligned to IEC 60060-1:2025 is a procurement differentiator, and ISO 11137-1:2025 revalidation cycles add sterilization-QA pull (L05-042). CN facility-RF upside (from C-20): CEPC-lineage efficiency economics (L05-012/013) and HEPS-class tender culture (L07-045) - upside channel only, subject to the existing CN-entity export partition.
competition_outlook_2030: US/EU 2030: ScandiNova (L05-047), Jema (L05-030), CPI (L05-050), plus OEM in-house. CN 2030: institute-affiliated suppliers (CAEP/CAS lineage, L05-014 context) and CGN in-house. No player currently sells an interoperable module family - that is the gap.
timing_window_risk: Export separability moderate: pulsed power is dual-use adjacent (radiography/EMP); keep high-energy variants US-only, CN entity ships industrial-class modules to civilian sterilization/NDT buyers - legal review required by 2028. Market risk: OEMs may insist on in-house drive chains; mitigate via performance/cost proof. Kill if no OEM design-in by end-2033.
extreme_edge: kA-class, <1% flat-top droop pulse synthesis from air-cooled solid-state stacks at half the cooling burden of legacy PFN systems (vendor-claim benchmark, L05-047).
first_experiment: 2027: two-module stack, 50kV/500A, 100us pulses at 1% droop with active correction; interoperability demo swapping modules mid-campaign; publish acceptance protocol per IEC 60060-1:2025. RF-drive line decisive experiment (from C-20): 20kW 500MHz GaN SSPA block with envelope tracking demonstrating >=68% chain efficiency into a cavity-equivalent load across 6dB backoff.
secondary_markets: KR: Vitzro Nextec/PAL klystron-localization ecosystem as manufacturing partner and validation site (L05-037) [from E-05]
founder_fit_note: Pulse-module control, digital droop correction, and standardized digital interfaces fit founder circuits, controls, and embedded skills, while 10-100 kV kA-class high-voltage packaging and insulation engineering sit at the edge of the profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed D-05 (open-interface standard - the differentiating move), E-05 (hot-swap <30min serviceability + KR Vitzro/PAL channel), A-07 (IEC 60060-1:2025 acceptance-protocol emphasis, Varex-class buyer detail), C-20 (SSPA RF-drive product family + its 20kW/>=68% decisive experiment), B-09 (3kV brick with per-stage droop correction; CGN/CIRC tender evidence already cited). China leg unchanged (already evidenced via L05-035/036); high-energy variants remain US-only per the export partition.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical modulator platform. Absorb D-05 open-interface standard (the differentiating move), E-05 hot-swap serviceability, A-07 acceptance protocol, C-20 SSPA line, B-09 brick detail.

concept: kW-class 20-80 K turbo-Brayton cryocoolers: gas-bearing modular cryogenic engines filling the gap between lab cryocoolers and bespoke helium plants
primary_customer_archetype: fusion magnet plants, HTS grid operators, quantum/science facilities
named_buyer_examples: Bluefors-era quantum facilities scaling cryo plant (US)
State Grid HTS cable operators (Shanghai line) (CN)
mid-scale national-lab/university magnet and accelerator programs (US, from D-03)
KEPCO/LS superconducting datacenter-grid lineage (KR side market, L03-043, from D-03)
IHEP-class Chinese facility programs (procurement style per HEPS tenders, L07-045; license-only channel, from B-11)
painful_job: US: fusion/HTS systems need kW-class 4-20K-adjacent cooling while commercial catalogs top out at mW-W scale (L03-050 vs the ~9.5kW-equivalent JT-60SA-class plants, L03-025-context); dilution-refrigerator cooling power is separately a documented quantum-scaling constraint (L07-014/015/016/017). CN: the operating Shanghai HTS cable and ASIPP's high-field program carry the same mid-scale cryogenic burden with bespoke plants (L03-041/052, L03-029).
product: A sealed turbo-Brayton cryocooler family: 200W-2kW at 20-80K, gas-bearing turboalternators, >5-year maintenance-free targets, rack-integrable - the missing product class between GM cryocoolers and custom helium refrigerators (the same machine class Nexans' SupraMarine demonstrator uses, L03-034). Product definition imported from merged duplicates D-03/B-11: catalog-priced skids with ~6-month lead times, N+1 redundancy, automated cooldown and remote monitoring - productized like industrial chillers, not engineered plants.
frontier_vision: Cryogenics as infrastructure: plug-in cold for HTS grids, fusion plants, hydrogen liquefaction nodes, quantum datacenters, and space simulators - the Carrier of the cold economy.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: turboalternator + gas-bearing research in a university turbomachinery lab; loss budget model; patents on bearing/alternator integration. 2028: decisive cold-stage demo (below). 2029: pilot LOI with one US fusion/HTS program; CN market entry study (licensing vs entity) with export-counsel review; refresh quantum-export-control scope. FIX: capex staged - component/cold-head work only until a named buyer LOI is in hand; no full-skid integration spend before that.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Fusion magnet factories, HTS grid pilots, and quantum buildouts all cross from lab scale to plant scale 2030-2034; every one hits the same cryogenic plant gap; incumbents (GM-cycle vendors, big-plant EPCs) leave the middle open.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: US: DOE fusion milestone follow-ons and magnet-factory expansions (L03-032/035); quantum facility scaling (Bluefors' US manufacturing base, L13-041, evidences localization pull). CN: HTS cable follow-on city deployments (L03-041/052) and ASIPP/CFETR magnet infrastructure (L03-029, L03-024).
competition_outlook_2030: Air Liquide/Linde turbo-Brayton at large scale and Cryomech/Sumitomo GM-class at small scale (L03-050) - the mid-band is thin; Stirling vendors adjacent. concept: Direct-DC renewable-to-datacenter coupling converters: multiport MVDC blocks that connect PV/BESS plants straight into 800VDC campuses without AC double conversion

concept: Electrolyzer stack degradation-emulation and bankability test systems: programmable ripple/intermittency benches that turn stack warranties into measurable engineering

Chinese EPC/owner acceptance-test labs for mega-tenders (CN)

concept: Merchant quench-detection-and-protection subsystem for REBCO/HTS magnets: co-wound distributed fiber-optic sensing plus sub-millisecond active energy extraction, sold with a qualification dossier to fusion companies and national labs.
named_buyer_examples: Commonwealth Fusion Systems (sells magnets to Realta, WHAM)
painful_job: HTS coils have very high minimum quench energy and slow normal-zone propagation, so voltage-tap detection fails; builders must choose between no-insulation windings (poor ramp/control) or unproven fast detection, over-engineering every multi-megajoule magnet (L03-004, L03-018, L03-020, L03-021).
product: Retrofittable quench detection + protection package: Rayleigh-backscatter fiber co-wound in the coil plus RF/acoustic hotspot channels, fused in a deterministic FPGA trigger driving a qualified multi-kV energy-extraction/CLIQ-class dump circuit; delivered with test data at 10-20 T relevant conditions. Merged extensions: delivered with a published acceptance-test protocol and warranty-support framing for merchant magnet sales (from A-04); the accumulated quench-precursor signature dataset across driven-quench campaigns is the compounding moat (from E-07).
frontier_vision: Become the certification-grade 'flight recorder and ejection seat' for every high-field HTS magnet -- fusion, NMR, accelerator, industrial -- the way rad-hard PLCs became mandatory in nuclear I&C.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university magnet-lab access (e.g., high-field magnet facility) and instrumentation of small REBCO pancakes; publish detection-latency benchmarks. 2027-28: DOE SBIR/INFUSE-class grants with a fusion partner; controlled-quench campaigns on 1-2 T coils. 2029: demonstrate 100 ms-class pre-quench warning plus automated dump on a multi-kA coil at a partner lab; freeze v1 architecture and qualification plan.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: SPARC-class results in 2026-27 convert into pilot-plant magnet builds around 2030; every new builder needs protection it cannot buy today, and qualification data accumulated 2027-2029 is the moat at exactly that moment.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Post-SPARC fusion pilot-plant magnet procurement (CFS ARC-class, Tokamak Energy ST80-HTS follow-on) and the DOE Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program renewal decision after its FY2027 authorization window (L03-032, L03-033, L03-035); CFETR magnet procurement if it opens (L03-024) is upside, not baseline. Side market (from E-07): KEPCO + LS Cable/LS Electric superconducting datacenter-grid MOU as a cable-monitoring channel (L03-042/043); US-ITER/ORNL magnet operations as lab-side buyers (L03-030). CN chapter (from C-17): ASIPP/CFETR demand logic exists (L03-024/029/037) but is served only via license-out and ONLY if export counsel clears - quench protection is dual-use-adjacent; no CN beachhead, flags unchanged.
competition_outlook_2030: In-house teams at CFS/TE Magnetics (L03-035, L03-044), national-lab solutions (CERN CLIQ lineage, tuned for LTS), instrumentation vendors without protection power electronics. No merchant vendor of an integrated HTS detection+dump product identified in the atlas.
timing_window_risk: If no-insulation windings prove fully sufficient at pilot-plant scale, the detection problem shrinks; if a magnet builder open-sources a good solution, differentiation collapses to service/qualification.
extreme_edge: Watching a superconductor start to die in real time -- centimeter-resolved strain/temperature along kilometers of tape at kilohertz rates -- and killing a 100 MJ discharge before it burns the coil.
first_experiment: Co-wind fiber + RF pickup in a 1-2 T REBCO double-pancake, drive controlled quenches, and demonstrate >=100 ms warning before thermal runaway with <1% false-trigger rate (2027, university magnet lab).
secondary_markets: JP: Furukawa/Fujikura REBCO ecosystem as conductor-QA partners (L03-044) [from E-07]
KR: KEPCO + LS Cable datacenter superconducting-grid MOU as cable-monitoring side market (L03-042, L03-043) [from E-07]
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: distributed fiber-optic sensing readout, multi-channel fusion in a deterministic FPGA trigger, and qualified energy-extraction electronics map directly onto founder instrumentation, embedded-systems, circuits, and data-acquisition skills.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-04 (acceptance-test protocol / warranty framing for merchant magnet sales; multi-modal sensing sources L03-005/006/007/008), C-17 (CN ASIPP/CFETR chapter - documented as license-out-only, counsel-gated; china_beachhead stays false), and E-07's protection half (precursor-dataset moat + KEPCO/LS cable-monitoring side market; its QA-bench half went to D-02).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical quench detection/protection. Falsifiable 2027 experiment; honest NI-winding substitution risk. Absorb A-04 protocol framing, E-07 precursor-dataset moat + KEPCO/LS side market; CN only if counsel clears.

concept: Reel-to-reel contactless REBCO tape quality metrology (Hall-array critical-current mapping + delamination screening) sold to tape vendors and magnet builders as certified acceptance instrumentation.

Shanghai Superconductor / Shanghai Shangchuang (CN domestic capacity buildout)

launch_2030_timing_thesis: Tape volume triples-to-10x between 2026 and the early-2030s pilot-plant builds; QC pain scales with volume and with new entrants' unproven lines, peaking exactly at launch.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Fusion pilot-plant tape procurement waves (CFS ARC-class; Tokamak Energy ST80-HTS wire deal as precedent, L03-044), Chinese domestic REBCO capacity expansion for import independence (L03-052), and CFETR magnet procurement if construction starts (L03-024). founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: Hall-array magnetic sensing, lock-in thermal imaging, and line-speed data acquisition with calibrated reporting are core founder sensors/instrumentation, electromagnetics, and numerical-analysis work.

concept: RF energy recovery for the world's klystron and gyrotron fleets: multi-stage depressed collector (MSDC) modules plus adaptive drive electronics that close the 12-point gap between simulated and hardware RF efficiency.

fusion ECH system integrators buying ITER-class gyrotrons

FIX additions: 2026-27 includes fleet-size and $-per-efficiency-point arithmetic from named facilities (SLAC-class linacs, ITER-class gyrotron stations) to bound the retrofit TAM; 2028: OEM/rebuilder channel agreement in principle (CPI-class); 2029 DECISION POINT: license the MSDC IP to tube OEMs or fold the drive-electronics line into P3R2-C-09 - a standalone company only if both the channel agreement and the arithmetic clear.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Facility electricity costs and decarbonization targets make efficiency retrofits fundable; rebuild cycles of aging tube fleets 2030-2034 are the natural insertion point without new-tube qualification risk.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: DOE GREEN-RF-style commercialization push (L05-032), fusion ECH procurement following the ITER/Thales gyrotron template as pilot plants order heating systems (L05-029), and collider-class efficiency programs (CEPC's documented ~RMB130M/yr savings case, L05-013 -- China demand logic, access-limited).
competition_outlook_2030: CPI (L05-050), Thales (L05-029), Canon (judgment), Vitzro Nextec (KR, L05-037) building their own high-efficiency tubes; labs doing one-off in-house collectors. Merchant retrofit modules with guaranteed uplift are unoccupied.
timing_window_risk: Invasive retrofits only work at rebuild; if OEMs internalize MSDC quickly the wedge narrows to drive electronics and test protocols.
extreme_edge: Catching a spent electron beam on a staircase of precisely biased electrodes and pushing its energy back into the wall plug.
first_experiment: MSDC prototype on a decommissioned S-band klystron at a lab test stand: demonstrate >=15-point wall-plug efficiency uplift vs baseline at 50%+ of rated output (2028).
founder_fit_note: Electron-optics simulation and drive-waveform electronics draw on founder electromagnetics, numerical-analysis, and circuits skills, but vacuum-tube fabrication, collector thermal design, and rebuild-shop integration are outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: named-facility fleet/$-per-efficiency-point arithmetic task added; 2028 OEM/rebuilder channel-agreement-in-principle milestone; explicit 2029 license-or-fold-into-C-09 decision. Licensing-shaped structure acknowledged (retrofit-at-rebuild + OEM internalization risk).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Non-obvious and documented gap, but retrofit-at-rebuild + OEM internalization makes it licensing-shaped.

concept: Compact solid-state-RF-driven e-beam/X-ray sterilization machine that breaks the IBA/L3 duopoly with a self-shielded, factory-installable unit for the ethylene-oxide phase-out.
painful_job: EtO replacement demand has the two pure-play e-beam accelerator makers (IBA Industrial, L3 Applied Technologies) backlogged for years, leaving medical-device and food producers unable to add sterilization capacity on schedule (L05-028); revised ISO 11137-1:2025 tightens radiation-sterilization validation (L05-042).
product: Self-shielded 10 MeV/30 kW-class e-beam (with X-ray conversion option) built on mass-produced GaN/LDMOS solid-state RF pallets instead of klystrons, shippable in standard modules, with dose-mapping automation aligned to ISO 11137-1:2025 out of the box. Deployment options (FIX, absorbing A-09): the same accelerator/RF core ships either as a central contract-sterilization machine or as a compact self-shielded factory-floor cell for device-OEM in-house sterilization (conveyor + integrated shielding + ISO 11137 dosimetry records) - acknowledging that per-site radiation licensing and contract-sterilization industry structure favor the central variant first.
frontier_vision: Sterilization as an appliance: distributed, per-factory beam processing replacing centralized gamma/EtO plants, then food-safety and materials-modification markets on the same platform.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: SSPA-driven accelerating-structure design with a university accelerator lab; regulatory mapping (ISO 11137/FDA pathways). 2027-28: 1 MeV/5 kW prototype beamline validating SSPA drive, gun, and dose uniformity. 2029: 10 MeV injector section + shielding/throughput engineering package; LOIs from two contract sterilizers. FIX gates: 2027 backlog-persistence check (kill if IBA/L3 clear backlogs and cut prices); 2028 SSPA-vs-klystron cost-crossover validation with vendor quotes; P4 re-source of the EtO regulatory driver from EPA NESHAP/FDA primaries (the current ledger lacks it).
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Working hypothesis with three load-bearing assumptions, each gated (FIX): (1) the EtO forcing function is durable regulatory pressure, not a one-cycle backlog - must be re-sourced from eligible primary documents (EPA NESHAP amendments / FDA sterilization guidance) in P4; (2) solid-state RF cost curves cross klystron economics before 2030 - validate with vendor quotes by 2028; (3) incumbent backlog persists - 2027 check: if IBA/L3 backlogs clear and prices fall, kill.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Continued EtO-replacement capacity build with duopoly backlog precedent (L05-028), ISO 11137-1:2025 revalidation cycles (L05-042), and cargo/industrial linac demand growth signaling accelerator supply tightness (L05-033); China's CGN Dasheng RMB340M order flow proves category economics (L05-035, CN market evidence -- CN treated as competitor territory, not beachhead).
competition_outlook_2030: IBA, L3 Applied Technologies (backlogged incumbents), Mevex (judgment), CGN Dasheng expanding from China (L05-035); Co-60 gamma remains substitute with its own supply constraints.
timing_window_risk: If incumbent backlogs clear by 2029 and they cut prices, a new entrant faces entrenched service networks; regulatory qualification timelines could push first revenue past 2031.
extreme_edge: A shipping-container linac: thousands of transistor watts phase-summed into a 10 MeV electron broom that sterilizes a pallet in seconds.
first_experiment: SSPA-driven 1 MeV/5 kW CW prototype beamline: demonstrate stable dose rate and RF-chain wall-plug efficiency >55% (2028, university accelerator facility).
founder_fit_note: Solid-state RF drive, dose-mapping automation, and machine controls fit founder circuits, controls, and instrumentation skills, while accelerator physics, radiation shielding, and sterilization regulatory validation are outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: EtO forcing function flagged for P4 re-sourcing from EPA NESHAP/FDA primaries; SSPA-vs-klystron cost crossover to be validated with quotes by 2028; 2027 backlog-persistence kill check added; A-09's self-shielded in-house cell absorbed as a deployment option (with its licensing-friction caveat).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Canonical sterilization machine; duopoly opening real but three load-bearing assumptions unproven.

concept: Charge-buildup-immune beam current and dose metrology for ultra-high-dose-rate (FLASH) and industrial electron beams -- the traceable ammeter the field admits it lacks.
named_buyer_examples: industrial e-beam sterilization operators needing ISO 11137 dose traceability
painful_job: Two independent 2024-2025 groups showed beam current transformers are corrupted by electric-field-driven charge buildup at FLASH dose rates -- an unresolved calibration/traceability problem for both clinical and industrial e-beam (L05-023, L05-024); sterilization validation standards meanwhile tighten (L05-042).
product: Hybrid non-intercepting monitor: cavity-resonator charge measurement cross-calibrated against an integrated Faraday/calorimetric reference, with metrology-grade uncertainty budget and drop-in form factors replacing legacy BCTs (Bergoz-class footprint). Merged extension (from A-08): a sterilization-QA product tier - per-unit calibration certificates for e-beam/X-ray plants validating under ISO 11137-1:2025 - alongside the clinical FLASH tier.
frontier_vision: Own the traceability layer for every high-dose-rate beam on earth -- clinical FLASH, sterilization, cargo scanning, semiconductor e-beam -- then expand into full beam-diagnostics suites.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: uncertainty-budget design and cavity prototype; beam time via university medical-physics collaborations. 2027-28: side-by-side campaign (cavity vs BCT vs Faraday cup) on a FLASH-capable linac, published. 2029: pre-production units at two beta sites (one clinical program, one industrial e-beam plant); NIST-traceable calibration chain drafted.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: FLASH clinical translation and e-beam sterilization expansion both hit regulatory dose-traceability walls around 2030; a validated instrument with published uncertainty is ready exactly when device makers must specify one.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: FLASH radiotherapy device qualification campaigns (pain documented in L05-023/024), EtO-replacement e-beam capacity requiring ISO 11137-1:2025-compliant dosimetry (L05-028, L05-042).
competition_outlook_2030: Bergoz Instrumentation (entrenched, published price list, L05-049), in-house lab diagnostics; incumbents optimized for storage rings, not UHDR transients.
timing_window_risk: Niche instrument market; if FLASH stalls clinically, industrial e-beam alone caps the market at tens of millions.
extreme_edge: Weighing 10^11 electrons in a microsecond without touching them, to a certified percent.
first_experiment: Side-by-side cavity monitor vs BCT vs Faraday cup at a FLASH-capable electron linac; quantify BCT error vs dose rate and demonstrate <1% cavity-monitor agreement with calorimetry (2027).
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: cavity-resonator charge measurement, Faraday/calorimetric cross-calibration, and metrology-grade uncertainty budgets are precision instrumentation and electromagnetics work at the center of the founder profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-08 (ISO 11137-1:2025 sterilization-QA channel and calibration-certificate product tier). C-10's CN leg NOT imported (mirrored assertion without CN procurement/regulatory evidence - 'fake dual' per adjudication); D-09 remains US-primary.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical UHDR metrology. Documented unresolved failure, cheap decisive experiment, honest small-TAM admission; pairs with C-09/D-08.

concept: Coherent-beam-combining phase-control engine: a channel-scalable electro-optic 'conductor' (controller ASIC/FPGA + modulator front-ends) sold to directed-energy primes and ultrafast-laser OEMs.
named_buyer_examples: nLIGHT Defense and Lockheed Martin Aculight (JLWS awardees)
painful_job: Transverse mode instability caps single-fiber power, making coherent combining of many channels the only path to 300-500 kW weapons and multi-kW ultrafast tools -- but multi-channel phase-locking, path-length and dispersion management remain bespoke, unsolved engineering rebuilt per program (L12-016, L12-017, L12-018, L12-001, L12-002).
product: Scalable phase-control stack: per-channel EO modulator front-ends, deterministic sub-microsecond controller (SPGD + interferometric hybrid) proven from 16 to 1,000+ channels, vibration-hardened, with open telemetry for system integration -- the subsystem every combiner needs and no one sells. Merged extension (from A-17): an environmental-grade deformable-mirror control and turbulence/jitter-compensation tier for 100kW-class systems, extending the phase-control engine from fiber arrays to full beam-control stacks.
frontier_vision: Be the Synopsys of coherent light: the standard control layer for laser weapons, ultrafast machining arrays, beamed power, and eventually phased optical apertures for space links.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: 16-channel testbed at a university photonics lab; lock to lambda/30 rms under acoustic disturbance. 2027-28: SBIR/STTR under DEW-adjacent topics; 128-channel demonstration with combining efficiency >90%. 2029: ruggedized brassboard evaluated by one prime under CRADA/sub-contract; ASIC path defined.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: JLWS transitions from initial award (2026) to scaled 300-500 kW fielding decisions in the early 2030s; primes under schedule pressure buy proven subsystems rather than rebuild controls, exactly when a 2029-validated brassboard exists.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: JLWS production scaling toward its $847M ceiling (L12-031, L12-032), DARPA AMPED/MELT tile architectures maturing into acquisition (L12-040, L12-041), and industrial CBC ultrafast systems entering machining markets (L12-002). Additional demand leg (from A-17): NNSA Expanded Yield Capability (EYC) execution beyond CD-1 (L12-042) and DARPA MELT/POWER follow-ons (L12-047).
competition_outlook_2030: Prime in-house control teams (nLIGHT, LM -- L12-033), Civan Lasers' integrated OPA approach (judgment), academic spinouts; no merchant phase-control vendor identified in the atlas.
timing_window_risk: Primes may classify and internalize controls; mitigation is dual-use industrial sales and ITAR-segregated product lines.
extreme_edge: A thousand lasers made to march in phase to a thirtieth of a wavelength -- an orchestra conducted at petahertz pitch with microsecond reflexes.
first_experiment: 16-channel fiber array phase-locked to lambda/30 rms with >90% combining efficiency under 1 g random vibration (2027).
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: per-channel electro-optic modulation, deterministic sub-microsecond FPGA control loops, and interferometric feedback are founder controls, embedded-systems, circuits, and signal-processing territory.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-17 (ruggedized deformable-mirror/adaptive-optics control tier; NNSA EYC demand leg L12-042 and MELT/POWER follow-ons L12-047; AO sources L12-009/010/011).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical CBC phase control; right abstraction level; dual-use hedge vs prime NIH; realistic channel-count ladder. Absorb A-17 DM-control + NNSA leg.
